concerns.text: T012 is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but explicitly states 'This task MUST run AFTER T011 completes'. A task that validates artifacts produced by a preceding task cannot be parallel to that producer. This violates the 'Producer before consumer' and '[P] correctness' rules.

T018 is marked [P] but depends on T015 (extracting thermal conductivity scalar) and T013 (computing metrics) to have the data available for the power analysis (n < 50 check). Furthermore, T018 modifies the Bonferroni logic used in T017. If T017 and T018 run in parallel, T017 may use the unadjusted alpha while T018 calculates the adjustment, leading to inconsistent statistical results. T018 must follow T015/T013 and precede or be integrated with T017.

T020 is marked [P] but the description states 'This task is a blocking prerequisite for the entire US3 chain; US3 cannot begin until T020 is complete.' A task that blocks the start of a story's subsequent tasks cannot be parallel to them. T020 must be sequential before T021, T022, etc.

The 'Parallel Opportunities' section claims T016, T017, and T018 can run in parallel. However, T018 (power analysis) determines the alpha threshold for T017 (Bonferroni correction). If T017 runs before T018 determines the adjusted alpha (if n<50), the correction is incorrect. T018 must precede T017 or be integrated into it.

The 'Parallel Opportunities' section states 'All Foundational tasks marked [P] can run in parallel (within Phase 2)'. However, T006 (Create data/metadata.yaml schema) logically depends on T002 (Initialize Python project) to ensure the environment exists to write the file, and T004a/b (utils/env config) often need to be present to handle the writing logic safely. While T001a-c are truly parallel, T002, T004, and T006 have implicit ordering dependencies not reflected in the [P] tags.
